<manually_attached_skills>
The user has manually attached the following skills to their message.
These skills contain specific instructions or workflows that the user wants you to follow for this request.
Only read the files if needed, the full skill content is inlined here.

Skill Name: babysit
Path: /home/vscode/.cursor/skills-cursor/babysit/SKILL.md
SKILL.md content:
# Babysit PR
Your job is to get this PR to a merge-ready state.

Check PR status, comments, and latest CI and resolve any issues until the PR is ready to merge.

1. Merge conflicts: Intelligently resolve any merge conflicts, preserving the intent and correctness of changes on your branch and the base branch. If intents conflict, abort the merge and ask for clarification.
2. Comments: Review active unresolved comments (including Bugbot) and resolve change requests / bug reports where valid. When fetching GitHub comments, filter out resolved threads first. Read only each comment body and the minimum location/URL needed to act on it; do not read the entire JSON output or other unnecessary payload data. Carefully validate issues reported by Bugbot and only take action on those that are valid; explain when you disagree or are unsure.
3. CI: Fix CI issues caused by changes within this PR's scope. Never change CI checks/workflows just to make failures pass, or make unrelated code changes; if that would be required, report back instead. For merge-blocking failures that seem unrelated to this PR, check whether the branch is behind the base branch and merge latest changes, since another PR may have fixed them. Push scoped fixes and re-watch CI until mergeable + green + comments triaged.
</manually_attached_skills>
<user_query>
can you reconcile this bad state with origin
</user_query>

Checking git state against origin to see what needs reconciling.

[REDACTED]

input.command: cd /workspaces/agno && git status && echo "---" && git branch -vv && echo "---" && git remote -v && echo "---" && git log --oneline -5 HEAD && echo "---" && git log --oneline -5 origin/$(git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD) 2>/dev/null || git fetch origin 2>&1 && git log --oneline -5 origin/$(git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD) 2>/dev/null
input.description: Inspect git status, branch tracking, recent commits

input.command: cd /workspaces/agno && git log --oneline origin/main..HEAD && echo "--- local only ---" && git log --oneline HEAD..origin/main && echo "--- remote only ---" && git show --stat e89f6de
input.description: Compare local vs origin main commits

input.command: cd /workspaces/agno && git ls-tree -d --name-only origin/main .rebaseai 2>/dev/null; echo "---"; ls -la .rebaseai 2>/dev/null | head -5 || echo "no .rebaseai locally"
input.description: Check if .rebaseai exists on origin vs locally

content.text:
Local `main` is one commit ahead (`remove old docs`) and two behind `origin/main`. Rebasing onto `origin/main` to reconcile.

[REDACTED]

input.command: cd /workspaces/agno && git fetch origin && git rebase origin/main
cd /workspaces/agno && git status && git log --oneline -5 && echo "---" && git log --oneline origin/main..HEAD
input.description: Verify main is reconciled with origin

content.text:
`main` was diverged from `origin/main`: your local branch had **1** commit (`remove old docs`) that wasn’t on the remote, and the remote had **2** commits you didn’t have (#208, #209).

I ran `git fetch` and `git rebase origin/main`. That replayed your cleanup commit on top of the latest remote history, so the branch is linear again and no longer “behind and ahead” in a conflicting way.

**Current state:**
- `main` is clean and based on `origin/main` (includes #208 and #209).
- You’re still **1 commit ahead** of `origin/main`: `991bc62 remove old docs` (deletes most of `.rebaseai/` artifact files; only `.rebaseai/pm` remains locally).

**Next steps (your choice):**
1. **Keep the cleanup** — push when ready: `git push origin main`
2. **Drop the local commit and match origin exactly** — `git reset --hard origin/main`

I didn’t push; say if you want that cleanup on `origin/main` or prefer a hard reset to match remote exactly.

[REDACTED]
